result.summary: OpenRouter exposes a unified authenticated HTTP API for LLM inference and account features, a hosted remote MCP server authenticated via browser OAuth, and official TypeScript/Python/Go client libraries plus a TypeScript agent SDK.
result.description: OpenRouter is an AI gateway that provides access to many language and multimodal models through a single API, with routing and fallback across providers. It also offers model discovery, account credit and key management, and tools for agent-oriented development.

openrouter_api_key.label: OpenRouter API key
openrouter_api_key.setup: Create a key from the [API keys page](https://openrouter.ai/keys) (or manage existing keys from [key settings](https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys)). Give it a name and optionally set a credit limit. Use the resulting key as a Bearer token in `Authorization`.

openrouter_management_key.label: OpenRouter Management API key
openrouter_management_key.setup: Create a management key from the [Management API Keys page](https://openrouter.ai/settings/management-keys): click **Create New Key** and complete the flow. This key is only for administrative key-management endpoints under `/api/v1/keys`, not for inference/completions.

openrouter_oauth_pkce_key.label: OpenRouter OAuth PKCE exchanged API key
openrouter_oauth_pkce_key.setup: For user-connect flows, send the user to [`https://openrouter.ai/auth`](https://openrouter.ai/auth) with `callback_url` and optional PKCE parameters. After consent, exchange the returned code at `POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/auth/keys` as described in the [OAuth PKCE guide](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/overview/auth/oauth). The response returns a user-controlled API key, which you then use as a Bearer token to call the API.
